---
title: "YOUR TITLE HERE"
subtitle: "STAT 642 — Spring 2026"
author: "Team 11"
date: "April 20, 2026"
format:
  beamer:
    theme: "Madrid"
    colortheme: "default"
    fonttheme: "structurebold"
    navigation: horizontal
    aspectratio: 169
    fontsize: 11pt
    header-includes:
      - \usepackage{booktabs}
---

## Outline

1. Introduction & Data Description
2. Research Questions
3. Model Specification & Assumptions
4. Analysis & Results
5. Assumption Checks
6. Conclusion

# Introduction & Data Description

## Data Source

- **Source:** TODO — describe where the data comes from
- **Sample size:** $n = $ TODO
- **Unit of observation:** TODO

## Variables

| Variable | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| $Y$      | Response (continuous) | TODO |
| $X_1$    | Predictor | TODO |
| $X_2$    | Predictor | TODO |
| $X_3$    | Predictor | TODO |

: Variables used in the analysis

## Experimental Design

<!-- If you designed the experiment, explain the design here. -->
<!-- Otherwise, describe how the data were collected. -->

- TODO — describe the study design or data collection process

# Research Questions

## What Is Interesting About These Data?

- TODO — explain what makes this dataset interesting or relevant

## Research Questions

1. TODO — state your main research question(s)
2. TODO
3. TODO

# Model Specification

## Model

$$
Y_i = \beta_0 + \beta_1 X_{1i} + \beta_2 X_{2i} + \beta_3 X_{3i} + \varepsilon_i
$$

- $Y_i$: TODO — response variable
- $\beta_0$: intercept
- $\beta_1, \beta_2, \beta_3$: regression coefficients for predictors
- $\varepsilon_i \overset{\text{iid}}{\sim} N(0, \sigma^2)$

## Assumptions

1. **Linearity** — the relationship between predictors and response is linear
2. **Independence** — observations are independent
3. **Normality** — errors are normally distributed
4. **Equal Variance** — constant error variance (homoscedasticity)

# Analysis & Results

## Exploratory Data Analysis

<!-- Include summary statistics, scatterplots, etc. -->

TODO — insert EDA plots and tables

## Model Fitting Results

<!-- Paste or include model output here -->

TODO — insert model summary output

## Hypothesis Tests

- **Overall F-test:** $H_0: \beta_1 = \beta_2 = \beta_3 = 0$
  - $F = $ TODO, $p = $ TODO
- **Individual t-tests:** TODO — report significant predictors

## Significant Differences

<!-- When H0 is rejected, clearly state which groups differ -->
<!-- or how variables increase/decrease -->

- TODO — describe the direction and magnitude of effects

# Assumption Checks

## Residual Plots

<!-- Include residual vs. fitted, Q-Q plot, etc. -->

TODO — insert residual diagnostic plots

## Addressing Violations

<!-- If assumptions are violated, explain remedial measures -->

- TODO — describe any transformations, alternative methods, etc.

# Conclusion

## Summary of Findings

- TODO — summarize the key results
- TODO — answer the research questions posed earlier

## Limitations & Future Work

- TODO — note any limitations of the analysis
- TODO — suggest directions for future investigation

## References

- TODO — cite data sources and methods as needed
